title: What is Graph Data Science?
text: Welcome to our exploration of Graph Data Science! In this lesson, we're diving into what makes Graph Data Science so unique and powerful. Let's get started!

title: GDS vs Graph data science
text: Graph data science can mean two things. It refers to the broader discipline of 'graph data science' and also to 'Graph Data Science' or 'GDS', which is a specific Neo4j plugin. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s002-001-existing] As you can see here, on the left we have GDS, and on the right, the broader concept of graph data science.

title: What You'll Learn
text: By the end of this lesson, you'll be equipped to distinguish between graph data science as a discipline and GDS as a Neo4j plugin. You'll also be able to explain how graph data science differs from traditional data science approaches, identify the key access points for using the GDS library, and understand when to use Aura Graph Analytics versus GDS.

title: What is Graph data science?
text: Graph Data Science is all about applying graph algorithms to graph data to analyze patterns in connected data. These algorithms are incredibly useful for cleaning your datasets and uncovering insights within them. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s004-001-existing] Take a look at this illustration. On the left, we see a graph with many connections, and on the right, the same graph with communities identified, labeled, and circled. This shows how graph-specific methods can reveal insights that would be hard to achieve with just tabular data.

title: How traditional data science works
text: Traditional data science focuses on finding patterns in attributes. For instance, you might look for users who have used the same card with the same ID as another user. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s005-002-existing] Here, you can see a table showing users in the left column and their cards in the right column.

title: How graph data science works
text: Graph data science, on the other hand, finds patterns in the connections between entities and their attributes. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s006-003-existing] This diagram shows a simple graph where a user node is connected to a card node, illustrating a basic relationship between entities in a graph database.

title: Example: Traditional fraud detection
text: In traditional fraud detection, the focus is on flagging individual suspicious accounts. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s007-004-existing] This table shows users in the left column and fraud flags, either 0 or 1, in the right column.

title: Example: Graph fraud detection
text: Graph data science takes fraud detection a step further by identifying coordinated networks of fraudsters working together. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s008-005-existing] Here, you see a peer-to-peer financial transaction network where two users are connected to the same cards, highlighting potential fraud detection scenarios.

title: Graph algorithms
text: The Neo4j Graph Data Science (GDS) library is packed with graph algorithms. It allows you to analyze your graph data through a simple, predictable, and endlessly repeatable framework. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s009-006-existing] Notice the three code blocks showing the steps to running a graph algorithm on connected data: Project, Run, and Write. The Run and Write steps are performed in the same command.

title: Access points
text: You can access the Graph Data Science library through several points: the Desktop plugin, the Aura plugin, and the GDS Python driver. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s010-007-existing] This image shows GDS in three contexts: Desktop Browser, Aura Browser, and GDS Python driver.

title: Aura Graph Analytics
text: Aura Graph Analytics is an on-demand service that lets you use the Graph Data Science library no matter where your source data is stored. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s011-008-existing] Here, you see the three steps to running a graph algorithm on connected data: Project, Run, and Write, with Run and Write performed in the same command.

title: Dedicated service
text: Aura Graph Analytics operates as a dedicated service in Aura, optimized for analytics workloads. It doesn't share memory or compute resources with your data store. While it functions similarly to GDS, Aura Graph Analytics is a distinct product. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s012-009-existing] This image emphasizes that Aura Graph Analytics is not GDS.

title: AGA via Python
text: Later in the course, you'll learn how to use Aura Graph Analytics via the Python driver. The workflow is only slightly different from using GDS. [IMAGE: img-1-foundations-1-what-is-gds-s013-010-existing] Here, two code blocks show the difference between running algorithms on Aura versus GDS through the Python client.

title: Lesson Summary
text: In this lesson, you learned to differentiate graph data science from traditional data science approaches. You also discovered what the GDS library is, what it does, and its various access points. In the next lesson, you'll learn the basic GDS workflow—a repeatable pattern you can reuse in any session.
